title: StatefulSet Rolling Update with Partition
description:
Use the `partition` field to perform a canary rollout — update only the highest-ordinal Pod first, then complete the rollout by clearing the partition.

**Focus**: The `partition` canary pattern is the safest way to roll out image changes to a StatefulSet running a distributed database.

## Common Pitfalls
- Forgetting to reset `partition: 0` leaves remaining Pods permanently on the old image.
- Setting `partition` equal to the replica count blocks ALL updates.
- Verify image with `kubectl get pod web-2 -o jsonpath='{.spec.containers[0].image}'`.
steps: Create a StatefulSet named 'web' with 3 replicas using image nginx:1.24 and a headless Service
Wait for all 3 pods to be Running
Set partition to 2 by patching: kubectl patch statefulset web -p '{"spec":{"updateStrategy":{"rollingUpdate":{"partition":2}}}}'
Update the image to nginx:1.25: kubectl set image statefulset/web nginx=nginx:1.25
Verify only web-2 has the new image; web-0 and web-1 still run nginx:1.24
Simulate canary validation passing, then clear the partition: kubectl patch statefulset web -p '{"spec":{"updateStrategy":{"rollingUpdate":{"partition":0}}}}'
Wait for all pods to be updated to nginx:1.25 and verify
hints: Check pod image: kubectl get pod web-2 -o jsonpath='{.spec.containers[0].image}'
kubectl rollout status statefulset/web shows rollout progress
partition: 2 means 'update pods with ordinal >= 2' — in a 3-pod set that's only web-2
kubectl patch is the quickest way to update a single field without editing the full manifest
After clearing partition, RollingUpdate proceeds web-1 then web-0 (descending order)

editor_config.initial_code:
# Headless Service
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: nginx
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  selector:
    app: nginx
  ports:
  - port: 80
---
# StatefulSet starting on nginx:1.24
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: web
spec:
  serviceName: "nginx"
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: nginx
  updateStrategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      partition: # TODO: Start with partition 0 (update all), we will change it
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: nginx
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: nginx
        image: # TODO: Start with nginx:1.24 (the 'old' version)
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80

_solution.code_solution:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: nginx
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  selector:
    app: nginx
  ports:
  - port: 80
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: web
spec:
  serviceName: "nginx"
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: nginx
  updateStrategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      partition: 0
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: nginx
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: nginx
        image: nginx:1.24
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
_solution.validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

kubectl apply -f solution.yaml

# Wait for all 3 pods on nginx:1.24
echo "Waiting for 3 pods on nginx:1.24..."
timeout=120
elapsed=0
while [ $elapsed -lt $timeout ]; do
  ready=$(kubectl get pods -l app=nginx --no-headers 2>/dev/null | grep -c Running || true)
  if [ "$ready" -ge 3 ]; then break; fi
  sleep 5; ((elapsed+=5))
done

for pod in web-0 web-1 web-2; do
  phase=$(kubectl get pod $pod -o jsonpath='{.status.phase}' 2>/dev/null || echo "Missing")
  if [ "$phase" != "Running" ]; then
    echo "✗ Pod $pod not Running before canary test"
    exit 1
  fi
done
echo "✓ All 3 pods Running on nginx:1.24"

# Set partition=2 and update image
kubectl patch statefulset web -p '{"spec":{"updateStrategy":{"rollingUpdate":{"partition":2}}}}'
kubectl set image statefulset/web nginx=nginx:1.25

# Wait for web-2 to update
echo "Waiting for web-2 to update to nginx:1.25..."
timeout=90
elapsed=0
while [ $elapsed -lt $timeout ]; do
  img=$(kubectl get pod web-2 -o jsonpath='{.spec.containers[0].image}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
  phase=$(kubectl get pod web-2 -o jsonpath='{.status.phase}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
  if echo "$img" | grep -q "1.25" && [ "$phase" = "Running" ]; then break; fi
  sleep 5; ((elapsed+=5))
done

# Verify canary: web-2 on new image, web-0/web-1 on old
web2_img=$(kubectl get pod web-2 -o jsonpath='{.spec.containers[0].image}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
if echo "$web2_img" | grep -q "1.25"; then
  echo "✓ web-2 updated to nginx:1.25 (canary)"
else
  echo "✗ web-2 image is '$web2_img', expected nginx:1.25"
  exit 1
fi

for pod in web-0 web-1; do
  img=$(kubectl get pod $pod -o jsonpath='{.spec.containers[0].image}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
  if echo "$img" | grep -q "1.24"; then
    echo "✓ $pod still on nginx:1.24 (partition respected)"
  else
    echo "✗ $pod image is '$img', expected nginx:1.24 (partition should block update)"
    exit 1
  fi
done

# Clear partition to complete rollout
kubectl patch statefulset web -p '{"spec":{"updateStrategy":{"rollingUpdate":{"partition":0}}}}'
echo "Partition cleared, waiting for full rollout..."
timeout=120
elapsed=0
while [ $elapsed -lt $timeout ]; do
  updated=0
  for pod in web-0 web-1; do
    img=$(kubectl get pod $pod -o jsonpath='{.spec.containers[0].image}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    phase=$(kubectl get pod $pod -o jsonpath='{.status.phase}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    if echo "$img" | grep -q "1.25" && [ "$phase" = "Running" ]; then
      ((updated+=1))
    fi
  done
  if [ "$updated" -ge 2 ]; then break; fi
  sleep 5; ((elapsed+=5))
done

for pod in web-0 web-1 web-2; do
  img=$(kubectl get pod $pod -o jsonpath='{.spec.containers[0].image}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
  if echo "$img" | grep -q "1.25"; then
    echo "✓ $pod on nginx:1.25 after full rollout"
  else
    echo "✗ $pod image is '$img' after full rollout, expected nginx:1.25"
    exit 1
  fi
done

echo "✓ All validations passed"
exit 0
